'''Robert John Smyth''' (born 22 February 1977) is an English [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a [[winger (rugby league)|winger]]. He played at club level for [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]], [[London Broncos]], [[Warrington Wolves]] and [[Leigh Centurions]] in the [[Super League]], and also represented [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] at international level.

==Career==
Smyth started his career with [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] before moving to [[London Broncos]] in 1998. While playing at London Broncos, Smyth kicked two conversions at Wembley in the [[1999 Challenge Cup]] Final defeat by [[Leeds Rhinos]].

In July 2000, Smyth joined [[Warrington Wolves]] on a one-month loan deal.<ref>{{cite web |title=Wolves Rope In A Bronco |url=https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/news/5293819.wolves-rope-in-a-bronco/ |website=Warrington Guardian |access-date=5 May 2023 |date=18 July 2000}}</ref> He scored four tries on his debut against [[Huddersfield-Sheffield Giants]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Smyth Sets Try Record |url=https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/news/5293535.smyth-sets-try-record/ |website=Warrington Guardian |access-date=5 May 2023 |date=27 July 2000}}</ref> He joined Warrington on a permanent deal at the end of the season.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Hadfield |first1=Dave |title=Elliott to exchange Bradford Bulls for Canberra |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/general/rugby-league/elliott-to-exchange-bradford-bulls-for-canberra-700427.html |website=The Independent |access-date=5 May 2023 |date=29 September 2000}}</ref>

Smyth was an [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] international.

Following retirement from the sport Smyth took a career in the construction industry as a Project Manager.  He currently works for Truline Construction & Interior Services Ltd in Wigan. <ref>{{Cite web |title=Truline {{!}} Commercial Interior Design & Construction Contractors |url=https://truline-cis.co.uk/ |access-date=2023-10-12 |language=en-GB}}</ref>
